criteria.name: Приветствие
criteria.description: Поприветствовал клиента, представился по имени, назвал свою должность
criteria.comment: Сотрудник не представился по имени и должности. Первое обращение ('Как мы к вам обращаемся?') зафиксировано за SPEAKER_01 (клиент по основной разметке), а в речи SPEAKER_02 приветствия и самопрезентации нет.

criteria.name: Имя клиента
criteria.comment: Сотрудник многократно использует имя клиента 'Андрей', например, при выяснении первого обращения и уточнении города.

criteria.name: Потребности
criteria.comment: Сотрудник выявляет потребности: интересуется, для себя ли машина, на каком авто ездит, планируется ли обмен и рассматривается ли кредит.

criteria.name: Ассортимент
criteria.description: Рассказал об общем ассортименте более 8 000 т/с и количестве т/с модели интересующей клиента
criteria.comment: Сотрудник не упоминал общий ассортимент компании (более 8 000 т/с) и количество автомобилей интересующей модели.

criteria.name: Диагностика
criteria.description: Сообщил, что все авто проходят комплексную диагностику
criteria.comment: Сотрудник сообщил, что каждый автомобиль проходит комплексную диагностику по 256 пунктам.

criteria.name: Без серьёзных ДТП
criteria.description: Рассказал, что к продаже не допускаются т/с после серьезных ДТП
criteria.comment: Сотрудник не произнёс прямое правило, что автомобили после серьёзных ДТП не допускаются к продаже. Он описал отсутствие повреждений у конкретной машины, но не заявил политику отбора.

criteria.name: Подъёмник
criteria.comment: Сотрудник неоднократно упоминает осмотр автомобиля на подъёмнике при визите.

criteria.name: Тест-драйв
criteria.description: В разговоре есть обсуждение или предложение тест-драйва
criteria.comment: Сотрудник обещает тест-драйв в день визита ('обязательно пройдем с вами тест-драйв').

criteria.name: Фокус на плюсах
criteria.description: Сотрудник больше говорит о плюсах, чем о минусах. Сотрудник рассказывает о минусах автомобиля только если просит клиент.
criteria.comment: Сотрудник делает акцент на преимуществах (диагностика, гарантия, видеопрезентация). Мелкие недостатки ('пару сколов') упоминает только в ответ на вопрос клиента о возможных повреждениях.

criteria.name: Гарантия ПТЗ
criteria.description: Рассказал о гарантии и условиях ПТЗ
criteria.comment: Понятие ПТЗ (продлённая техническая защита) не озвучено и её условия не разъяснялись. Упоминается 'дополнительная гарантия' без расшифровки сроков, покрытия и стоимости.

criteria.name: Представление
criteria.description: Сотрудник упомянул в разговоре 2 преимущества из списка (компания более 20 лет на рынке; компания представлена в 40 городах страны и является официальным дилером 10 марок; компания продает более 20 000 авто в год; все авто проходят диагностику по 258 пунктам; На все авто предоставляется юридическая и техническая гарантия)
criteria.comment: Сотрудник привёл как минимум два преимущества из списка: компания работает более 20 лет и все автомобили проходят диагностику по 256 пунктам.

criteria.name: Обмен авто
criteria.description: В разговоре есть обсуждение или предложение услуги обмена автомобиля
criteria.comment: Сотрудник прямо спрашивает о планах на обмен и позже говорит о дополнительных выгодах при trade‑in, обсуждая способ оплаты.

criteria.name: Способ оплаты
criteria.description: Уточняет способ оплаты кредит или наличные
criteria.comment: Сотрудник уточняет способ оплаты: рассматривает ли клиент кредит, свои ли наличные.

criteria.name: Готовность к визиту
criteria.description: Спросил, готов ли клиент к визиту и осмотру автомобиля сегодня
criteria.comment: Сотрудник предлагает приехать сегодня и посмотреть автомобиль 'в живую'.

criteria.name: Телефон
criteria.comment: Сотрудник просит продиктовать номер телефона для отправки контактов и адресов.

criteria.name: Фото/видео
criteria.comment: Сотрудник предлагает записать видеообзор автомобилей, чтобы подтвердить их существование и состояние ЛКП.

criteria.name: Дата визита
criteria.description: Договорился о конкретном дне и времени визита
criteria.comment: Договорились на завтра утром, сотрудник просит предварительно позвонить перед приездом.

criteria.name: Приглашение коллег
criteria.description: Уточнил не осталось ли вопросов у клиента
criteria.comment: Сотрудник не задавал клиенту вопрос об оставшихся вопросах; инициатива исходила от клиента.

speaker_roles_reasoning: SPEAKER_02 предоставляет информацию о компании, диагностике, условиях покупки, организует визит; SPEAKER_01 задаёт вопросы о конкретном автомобиле, называет своё имя Андрей и обращается к сотруднику как Максим. Несмотря на единичные ошибки маркировки, роли однозначны.
reasoning_trace:
We are given a transcript of a dialog with audio features, SER labels, and summaries. We need to identify which speaker is the employee (the one we evaluate) and which is the client. Then evaluate the specified criteria only for the employee, using textual content, audio features, and SER as hints. We must output JSON with roles, reasoning, and criteria assessment.

Let's first determine roles based on the dialog content. The dialog involves a conversation about buying a car (Kia Ceed). The customer is asking about the car, its condition, price, credit, trade-in, test drive, etc. The employee is the one answering questions, providing information about the company, car diagnostics, financing options, etc.

Looking at the transcript:
- Initially, speaker 01 says: "Как мы к вам обращаемся?" (How do we address you?) - This is like a greeting, asking the customer's name. That seems like the employee.
- Then speaker 01 says "Здравствуйте, меня Андрей зовут." - This is likely the customer introducing himself. Wait, the first line is speaker 01 asking "Как мы к вам обращаемся?" That's likely the employee asking the customer's name. Then speaker 01 says "Здравствуйте, меня Андрей зовут." If this is the same speaker (01) then it's inconsistent: the same person asks and then answers? Actually, the transcript segments: [0.00–1.20] SPEAKER_01: Как мы к вам обращаемся? This is likely the employee talking. But the next segment is [1.86–3.00] SPEAKER_01: Здравствуйте, меня Андрей зовут. That would mean SPEAKER_01 is both employee and customer, which is impossible. So there must be a mislabeling or the text was transcribed with some speaker changes not correctly tracked. Alternatively, the segment [0.00–1.20] might be a continuation of a longer recording where the employee greeted, but here it's the customer asking? Let's analyze.

We have two distinct speakers. The dialog: 
First, someone says "Как мы к вам обращаемся?" - this is a plural "we" likely meaning "How should we address you?" from a company representative. Then someone says "Здравствуйте, меня Андрей зовут." This is a response giving the name. Then "Андрей, очень приятно. Какой автомобиль вас заинтересовал?" This is the employee again, addressing the customer by name and asking about the car. Then "Kia Ceed, меня интересует, у вас в красном цвете." This is the customer stating his interest. Then "Так, Kia Ceed в красном цвете, отлично. Себе автомобиль подбираете?" This is the employee echoing and asking about purpose. Then short responses: "Себе." and then "Для себя. А сейчас в каком автомобиле ездите?" (employee asking about current car). Then "Какая разница?" (customer annoyed) and then "На обмен планируете?" (employee asking about trade-in). Then "Нет, за наличные планирую." (customer). Then "За наличные. Кредит не рассматриваете? У вас наличные свои?" (employee asking about payment method). Then "Свои." (customer). Then "Свои, отлично." (employee). Then "У нас KIA Ceed 2018 года, 123 тысячи пробегов, все верно?" (employee confirming car details). Then "Да, все верно, Максим." (customer - here he addresses the employee as Максим, which is likely the employee's name). So the employee is Максим, and the customer is Андрей. So the employee is the one who later says "Расскажи немного о нашей компании." Wait, that line: [37.34–38.74] SPEAKER_02: Расскажи немного о нашей компании. So SPEAKER_02 says "Расскажи немного о нашей компании." (Tell a little about our company). That would be a command to the other speaker to tell about the company. If the employee is Максим, then the employee would be giving info about the company. So who is SPEAKER_02? Let's trace.

From earlier, the employee addressed the customer as "Андрей, очень приятно. Какой автомобиль вас заинтересовал?" That was [3.38–5.80] SPEAKER_01. So SPEAKER_01 seems to be the employee. Then later, the customer says "Да, все верно, Максим." That is [32.54–33.32] SPEAKER_02? No, [32.54–33.32] is SPEAKER_02 saying "Да, все верно, Максим." Actually the segment says: [32.54–33.32] SPEAKER_02: Да, все верно, Максим. So SPEAKER_02 is the customer, because he's agreeing and addressing the employee as Максим. Then employee continues: [33.64–35.22] SPEAKER_02: Андрей, вы впервые к нам обращаетесь? Wait, after the customer said "Да, все верно, Максим", the next line is SPEAKER_02: Андрей, вы впервые к нам обращаетесь? That would mean SPEAKER_02 is both the customer and employee? That's inconsistent. Let's re-check the timestamps carefully:

[27.92–32.40] SPEAKER_02: У нас KIA Ceed 2018 года, 123 тысячи пробегов, все верно? | ... This is the employee (the one saying "у нас", meaning "we have"). So SPEAKER_02 is the employee. Then [32.54–33.32] SPEAKER_02: Да, все верно, Максим. | Wait, that would mean SPEAKER_02 is saying "Да, все верно, Максим" and also the previous line. But the employee asking "все верно?" and the customer replying "Да, все верно, Максим" are two different lines. So [32.54–33.32] should be the customer. The transcript labels [32.54–33.32] as SPEAKER_02, but that seems to be a mistake; likely it's SPEAKER_01. Or the reverse: earlier, the employee was SPEAKER_01, and then a switch occurred. Let's parse the entire transcript more systematically:

List all segments with speaker and content:

[0.00–1.20] SPEAKER_01: Как мы к вам обращаемся? -> employee (greeting)
[1.86–3.00] SPEAKER_01: Здравствуйте, меня Андрей зовут. -> customer introducing himself (so this should be customer; but it says SPEAKER_01. So maybe there was no speaker change? Actually, the first two lines are both SPEAKER_01, but they are contradictory: one is asking "how to address you?" and the next is answering. This could be a transcription error, or the second line is actually the customer but mislabeled. Alternatively, the employee might be saying both lines: asking and then giving his own name? "Как мы к вам обращаемся? Здравствуйте, меня Андрей зовут." That would be weird. But then the next line [3.38–5.80] SPEAKER_01: Андрей, очень приятно. Какой автомобиль вас заинтересовал? So the same speaker is addressing himself? So clearly the second line is not the same speaker as the first. The most plausible is that the speaker labels in the transcript have swapped at times. I need to infer based on content.

I'll assign identities based on context:
- The employee: asks "Как мы к вам обращаемся?" (initial contact). Then later asks about car interest, asks about trade-in, offers credit, describes diagnosis, offers test drive, asks for phone number, schedules visit, etc.
- The customer: provides name (Андрей), asks about specific car (Kia Ceed, red), says "какая разница?" (annoyed), says pays in cash, asks about discount, asks about car condition (крашеный? and etc.), says he can't come today, etc.

Now, who says "Расскажи немного о нашей компании." at [37.34–38.74]? That could be the customer asking the employee to tell about the company. So the employee would be the one who then tells about the company history, diagnostics, etc. That segment is [38.92...] SPEAKER_02: На автомобильном рынке уже более 20 лет... So SPEAKER_02 is the employee responding. So the line "Расскажи немного о нашей компании" is spoken by the customer (thus not SPEAKER_02, but the transcript says SPEAKER_02). That indicates that sometimes the labeling might be wrong. However, the overall pattern: SPEAKER_02 is consistently the one providing detailed info about the company, the car, the diagnostics, the credit, etc. So SPEAKER_02 is the employee. The customer is SPEAKER_01.

Let's verify by examining lines that clearly fit roles:
- [9.66–12.84] SPEAKER_02: Так, Kia Ceed в красном цвете, отлично. Себе автомобиль подбираете? -> This is employee, responding to the customer's interest. Good.
- [13.22–13.74] SPEAKER_02: Себе. -> short answer "For myself". This would be the customer, but labeled SPEAKER_02. So contradiction. Possibly the speaker labels were swapped by ASR. But then [14.34–16.96] SPEAKER_02: Для себя. А сейчас в каком автомобиле ездите? This is again the employee asking a question. So after the customer says "Себе.", the next line is "Для себя. А сейчас..." That could be the employee's elaboration: "Для себя. А сейчас в каком автомобиле ездите?" That is plausible: the employee repeats "For yourself" and then asks a question. So [13.22–13.74] could be the employee summarizing: "Себе." Not ideal but possible. Actually, the customer previously said "Kia Ceed, меня интересует, у вас в красном цвете." Then employee: "Так, Kia Ceed в красном цвете, отлично. Себе автомобиль подбираете?" So the employee is asking "Are you choosing for yourself?" The customer answers "Себе." That should be the customer. But the transcript shows SPEAKER_02 saying "Себе." This would mean the employee is answering his own question. That is unlikely. More likely, the ASR or segmentation incorrectly merged speakers. But we have to work with what we have. The safest bet is to assign SPEAKER_02 as the employee because they provide all the information, asking for details, and later the customer calls him "Максим". At [32.54–33.32] "Да, все верно, Максим." that line is labeled SPEAKER_02, but that's the customer addressing the employee by name. So again, inconsistency. But the line [33.64–35.22] SPEAKER_02: Андрей, вы впервые к нам обращаетесь? That is the employee addressing the customer as "Андрей". So after the customer says "все верно, Максим", the employee asks "Андрей, вы впервые к нам обращаетесь?" So the labeling is erroneous, but overall the pattern of content is: 
   The employee's speech includes phrases like "у нас", "наша компания", "наша диагностика", "предоставляем гарантию", "адреса", "контакты". The customer's speech includes questions about car, name, "Андрей зовут", "Киа Сид", "за наличные", "свои", "разница", "расскажи о компании", "адрес актуален", etc.
Checking later: [65.48–70.62] SPEAKER_01: расскажите мне максим про него в каком он состоянии цена такая просто низкая достаточно -> customer asking employee (Максим) about car condition. So SPEAKER_01 is the customer. Then [70.62–74.98] SPEAKER_01: он битый наверно крашеный да сейчас посмотрим -> customer's own musing. Then [74.98–80.80] SPEAKER_02: так здесь 18 год с какого города Андрей к нам -> employee asking where from. So indeed: SPEAKER_01 = customer, SPEAKER_02 = employee. The earlier confusion is just mislabeling in the early part (the name introduction might have been the customer, but labeled SPEAKER_01; but then the employee's first "Как мы к вам обращаемся?" is also SPEAKER_01, so that means the first line (employee) and the second line (customer) are both attributed to SPEAKER_01. This can be a diarization error where they didn't split speakers correctly. But the later consistent pattern after [9.66] shows that essential employee talk starts with SPEAKER_02. The employee might have been labeled as SPEAKER_02 for most of the dialog. So we'll set SPEAKER_02 as EMPLOYEE, SPEAKER_01 as CLIENT.

We'll need to output roles and confidence. Provide reasoning: "SPEAKER_02 provides company information, asks diagnostic and financial questions, schedules visit; SPEAKER_01 asks about car features, responds with personal details, and addresses employee as Максим."

For the criteria, we must evaluate only the employee (SPEAKER_02). We'll go through each criterion using the transcript, audio features, SER hints, and any text content. We'll also consider the acoustic features for involvement: the employee's audio shows mostly monotone_pitch, warm_dark_timbral, with F0σ range from 5.4 to 22.4 Hz (some expressive moments). The SER mostly "нейтральный", which in this context is conversational, not unengaged. The overall acoustic profile: SPEAKER_02 has lower mean pitch (102 Hz) and F0σ 13.33 Hz, while SPEAKER_01 has higher (109 Hz) and 18.23 Hz. The employee's speech has monotone_pitch flags in many segments, but some segments without that flag, e.g., [80.80–89.92] warm_dark_timbral, no monotone_pitch, F0σ=22.4 Hz (more expressive). So that suggests some variation. Not all criteria rely on audio; many are textual.

Let's evaluate each criterion:

1. **greeting** (Приветствие, вес 1): Поприветствовал клиента, представился по имени, назвал свою должность
   Employee: Did they greet and introduce themselves by name and position? The dialog starts with SPEAKER_01 (likely mislabeled as employee) asking "Как мы к вам обращаемся?" which is a greeting but no name and position. Then later, when the employee is clearly SPEAKER_02, he says "Так, Kia Ceed в красном цвете, отлично. Себе автомобиль подбираете?" That is not a greeting. There is a line [33.64–35.22] SPEAKER_02: Андрей, вы впервые к нам обращаетесь? That is a question, not a greeting. So I don't see a proper greeting where the employee says hello, I'm so-and-so, position. The first line from the employee (if SPEAKER_01 first line is actually the employee) is just "Как мы к вам обращаемся?" which is a way to ask for the customer's name. But no introduction of employee himself. So I think greeting criteria not met. Could there be an earlier part missing? The transcript starts at 0.00 with that line, so likely did not greet properly. Therefore passed=false. Comment: "Сотрудник не представился по имени и должности, только запросил имя клиента." Evidence segments: perhaps the first segment [0.00–1.20] if we consider that employee line, but it's ambiguous. Since we assign SPEAKER_02 as employee, the greeting line is actually from a different speaker mislabeled. So no greeting by the actual employee. We'll mark false and note that.

2. **name_usage** (Имя клиента, вес 1): Уточнил имя клиента
   The employee (SPEAKER_02) uses the customer's name "Андрей" several times: at [33.64–35.22] "Андрей, вы впервые к нам обращаетесь?", at [74.98–80.80] "с какого города Андрей к нам", etc. So yes, he used the name. He also initially asked "Как мы к вам обращаемся?" which is name gathering. So passed=true. Evidence: [0.00–1.20] (if employee line), and [33.64–35.22], etc. Since the first line might be the employee, we can include it. The name was also probably obtained in the initial interaction.

3. **needs_assessment** (Потребности, вес 1): Выявление потребностей
   Employee asks several questions to assess needs: "Какой автомобиль вас заинтересовал?" (car interest), "Себе автомобиль подбираете?" (for yourself?), "А сейчас в каком автомобиле ездите?" (what do you drive now?), "На обмен планируете?" (trade-in?), "Кредит не рассматриваете?" (credit?). So needs assessment is present. passed=true. Evidence segments: [9.66–12.84], [14.34–16.96], [18.84–19.66]? Actually [19.98–21.00] "На обмен планируете?", [22.88–25.46] "Кредит не рассматриваете? У вас наличные свои?". So multiple.
